narrative:
Cleveland City has 238,663 registered voters, with 63.8% unaffiliated or lacking primary history. Republican-leaning voters make up 3.6% of registrants and Democratic-leaning voters account for 32.5%, a pronounced Democratic lean of 28.9 percentage points. Registration is trending redder in younger age cohorts (1990s–2000s births lean +10.5% D−R vs +44.8% for 1950s–60s cohorts).
